intro: 105 AI items this week (72 India-relevant) across 9 sources.

items.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
items.summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

items.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
items.summary: Here's what you need to do to get those pesky "write with Gemini" pop-ups to go away.

items.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to steal 2FA code from users
items.summary: SearchLeak exploit shows why the industry's approach to LLM security fails over and over.

items.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
items.summary: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

items.title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China, but how?
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: MosaicLeaks: Can your research agent keep a secret?
From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot
Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
items.summary: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

items.title: In the Weights is your new AI-centric vanity search
items.summary: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

items.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
items.summary: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

items.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
items.summary: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails. Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an…

items.title: The CEO of Allbirds’ new AI biz has a plan, but no team
items.summary: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.

items.title: UnitedHealth’s $3 billion AI push has bots calling doctors
items.summary: The largest US health insurer plans to invest $3 billion in AI over 2026 and 2027. UnitedHealth executives say they’re seeing a 2-to-1 return, as AI automates cumbersome manual processes and makes workers more efficient. Executives say the technology can reduce friction for pa…

items.title: AI, AI Sir! Indian execs back in the classroom for a fresh chapter

items.title: Trump tells Axios he no longer views Anthropic as national security threat
items.summary: Senior Anthropic technical staff were scheduled to meet with Trump administration ‌officials earlier ⁠this week ⁠to discuss a dispute over foreign access to its most advanced ​AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. The company last week disabled access ​for all users to those models…

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: ai-weekly-report
repos.desc: My personalized AI newsletter that runs on an agentic AI

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.
